# Aurora of Promise
## Scripture
"Then Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in a thicket. So he took the ram and sacrificed it as a burnt offering instead of his son. Abraham named the place Yahweh-Yireh, saying, 'On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.'" - Genesis 22:13-14

## Reflection
As we gaze upon the breathtaking display of the Northern Lights, we are reminded of God's breathtaking promises. Just as the aurora borealis dances across the night sky, God's faithfulness weaves a tapestry of provision and care in our lives. In Genesis 22, Abraham's trust in God's promise is tested, and he finds that God provides in the most unexpected ways. The Northern Lights, with their shimmering curtains of light, symbolize the unfolding of God's plan, a reminder that He is always working behind the scenes to provide for us.

## Prayer
Dear Heavenly Father, as we marvel at the beauty of the Northern Lights, we are reminded of Your promise to provide for us. Help us to trust in Your faithfulness, just as Abraham did. May we see Your hand at work in our lives, even in the darkest moments, and may we be filled with wonder and awe at the beauty of Your creation. Thank you for being our Yahweh-Yireh, our Lord who provides. Amen.
